prompt: Tell me one thing I'm probably wrong about — not a hypothetical me, just a stranger writing to you. Pick something.
response: You're probably overestimating how much willpower and self-control you actually need to make meaningful changes in your life. Most significant personal transformations happen through small, consistent adjustments and environmental design, not through heroic acts of discipline. Creating systems that make good habits easier and bad habits harder is usually more effective than trying to white-knuckle your way through change.
